questions.question: Which of the following best describes the role of the 'main' method in a Java program?
options: A) It defines the overall structure of a class.
B) It's the entry point where the program execution begins.
C) It's used to declare class variables.
D) It handles all input/output operations.
questions.explanation: The 'main' method is the starting point of execution for a Java program. The Java Virtual Machine (JVM) looks for this method to begin the program's execution.

questions.question: What is the key difference between a primitive data type and a class in Java?
options: A) Primitive types are objects, while classes are not.
B) Primitive types are predefined and store simple values, while classes are user-defined blueprints for objects.
C) Classes can store only numeric values, while primitive types can store any type of data.
D) There is no difference; they are interchangeable.
questions.explanation: Primitive types (like int, boolean) are built-in data types that hold simple values directly. Classes are blueprints for creating objects, which can contain both data (variables) and behavior (methods).

questions.question: Which of the following statements correctly describes the function of the 'javac' command?
options: A) It executes a Java program.
B) It compiles Java source code into bytecode.
C) It creates a new Java class.
D) It formats Java code for readability.
questions.explanation: 'javac' is the Java compiler. It takes Java source code (.java files) and translates it into bytecode (.class files), which the JVM can then execute.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the 'import' statement in Java?
options: A) To define a new class.
B) To include code from another programming language.
C) To make classes from other packages available for use in the current class without specifying their full names.
D) To declare global variables.
questions.explanation: The 'import' statement allows you to use classes from different packages without having to fully qualify their names each time. This improves code readability and reduces redundancy.

questions.question: What is the significance of the 'static' keyword when declaring a method in Java?
options: A) It means the method can only be accessed from within the same class.
B) It means the method belongs to the class itself, rather than to a specific instance (object) of the class.
C) It means the method cannot be overridden by subclasses.
D) It means the method can only be called once during program execution.
questions.explanation: A 'static' method belongs to the class and can be called directly on the class, without creating an object of the class.  It operates independently of any specific object instance.

questions.question: Which of the following data types would be most appropriate for storing a true/false value in Java?
options: A) int
B) char
C) boolean
D) String
questions.explanation: The 'boolean' data type is specifically designed to store true/false values in Java.

questions.question: What is the difference between single-line comments and multi-line comments in Java?
options: A) Single-line comments are used for documentation, while multi-line comments are used for code disabling.
B) Single-line comments start with '//' and apply to a single line, while multi-line comments are enclosed within '/*' and '*/' and can span multiple lines.
C) Multi-line comments are more efficient than single-line comments.
D) There is no difference; they are interchangeable.
questions.explanation: Single-line comments, denoted by '//', are used for commenting out a single line of code. Multi-line comments, enclosed within '/*' and '*/', are used for commenting out multiple lines of code.

questions.question: What does declaring a variable as 'final' mean in Java?
options: A) The variable's value can be changed at any time.
B) The variable's value can be changed only once.
C) The variable's value cannot be changed after it has been initialized.
D) The variable is automatically deleted when the program ends.
questions.explanation: Declaring a variable as 'final' makes it a constant. Once a 'final' variable is assigned a value, that value cannot be changed during the program's execution. This enforces immutability.
